summary: Large decompilation pass for Link arrow item code, replacing an effectively empty `itlinkarrow.c` with about 932 lines and bringing the file to the PR-stated 79% match. The PR added Link arrow item state functions, spawning/update helpers, per-item variable and attribute structs, typed prototypes needed by the implementation, and adjusted Link's neutral-special arrow position update to pass both current and source positions. There were no PR body notes or review comments in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Implemented `src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c` from a near-empty file into a large item module with `ItemStateTable it_803F6A28[5]`, numeric tables `numbers` and `numbers_l`, and many address-named functions from `it_802A7D8C` through `it_802AA034`.

Added `itLinkArrowVars` and `itLinkArrowAttributes` to `itCharItems.h`, with offset-style fields, `Vec3` history slots, `Quaternion` history slots, auxiliary `HSD_JObj*` storage, owner/fighter object pointers, and special-attribute joints.
Added `linkarrow` to the `Item` union in `src/melee/it/types.h`, allowing the new implementation to access `item->xDD4_itemVar.linkarrow`.
Expanded `itlinkarrow.h` with typed prototypes for the Link arrow functions and moved the `it_802A8398`/`it_802A83E0` declarations out of the generic `it_27CF.h` bucket.

Changed `it_802A8398` from taking only an item object and one `Vec3*` to taking an item object plus two `Vec3*` values; the implementation stores the first into `item->pos` and the second into `linkarrow.x18`.
Updated four Link neutral-special animation call sites in `ftLk_SpecialN.c` to include `itlinkarrow.h` and pass both `&vecs.b` and `&vecs.a` to `it_802A8398`.

Typed `ftCo_80094098` as returning `f32`/`float` and taking `HSD_GObj*` plus `f32*`/`float*`, replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, because Link arrow code calls it while handling an attached/reflected state.
Added needed item helper declarations: `it_8026EA20(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, Vec3*, Vec3*, s32*, UNK_T)` in `it_266F.h` and `it_802787B4(Item_GObj*, s32)` in `it_2725.h`.
The diff also introduced an apparent duplicate declaration of `it_80277040(Item_GObj*)` in `it_2725.h`; the slice has no review discussion explaining whether this was intentional.
smart_moves: The implementation first created concrete Link arrow variable and attribute layouts, then threaded them into the central `Item` union, enabling field accesses to stay consistent across the large item state machine.
The PR narrowed previously unknown prototypes where the new code had strong call-site evidence, especially `ftCo_80094098`, instead of leaving broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` signatures in place.
Moving Link-arrow declarations from `it_27CF.h` to `itlinkarrow.h` made the ownership of those symbols clearer and let `ftLk_SpecialN.c` include the specific item header it uses.

The two-position update for `it_802A8398` preserved both arrow display position and another tracked Link-arrow point (`linkarrow.x18`), which matches the new implementation's later use of position history and trail/transform data.

The file keeps explicit TODO-marked `fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation`, `fake_HSD_JObjSetScale`, and `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` shims with a note to remove them once `lbcoll` is matched, documenting a temporary matching dependency rather than hiding it.
Several inline helpers and stack-padding locals are isolated around matching-sensitive sections, making it easier for future work to remove or adjust them without rewriting the item logic.
decomp_lessons: For large item modules, establish the item-specific `xDD4_itemVar` struct early; even offset-named fields are useful when they expose repeated `Vec3`, `Quaternion`, owner, and auxiliary JObj patterns.

When an item article is controlled by a fighter action, verify the fighter-side call signatures at the same time as the item implementation; here Link's neutral-special code needed to pass both relevant positions to the arrow item.
Do not leave generic `UNK_PARAMS` prototypes if the newly matched caller proves argument and return types; tightening one helper signature can unblock matching in another subsystem.
Temporary baselib/HSD shims can be acceptable matching tactics when clearly marked with a removal condition, but follow-up work should revisit them once the underlying library functions are matched.
Address-named functions can still be organized through item-specific headers and state tables; the file-level ownership matters even before semantic names are known.
Watch for accidental header churn in matching PRs: the duplicate `it_80277040` declaration is searchable evidence for a future cleanup check.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as `it_802A7D8C`, `it_802A83E0`, `it_802A850C`, and `it_802A9D4C`.
Item-specific structs use semantic type names with offset fields: `itLinkArrowVars` and `itLinkArrowAttributes` with members like `x18`, `x94`, `xB4`, and `xE0`.
The central `Item` union member uses lowercase item naming: `item->xDD4_itemVar.linkarrow`.
Typed object aliases are preferred in prototypes where known: `Item_GObj*`, `Fighter_GObj*`, `HSD_GObj*`, `Fighter_Part`, `Vec3*`, and `f32`.
Header comments retain address tags, for example `/* 2A8398 */ void it_802A8398(...)` and `/* 3F6A28 */ extern ItemStateTable it_803F6A28[];`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used pointer arithmetic over adjacent `Vec3` and `Quaternion` fields, such as `*(&linkarrow.x48 - i)` and `*(&linkarrow.x84 - i)`, to match compact history-shift code generation.
Kept `#if 1`/`#else` alternatives around a history-copy sequence, showing a matching-specific loop form alongside a clearer expanded assignment form.
Introduced `#define INLINE 0` and an inline random-angle helper variant for `fn_802A81C4`, preserving a matching-tuned version that indexes floats relative to `it_803F6A28`.
Used explicit stack padding locals (`f32 pad[...]`, `UNK_T sp60`) and carefully placed temporaries in several functions to influence stack layout.
Preserved exact floating-point constants and math calls such as `deg_to_rad`, `1.5707963267948966`, `3.141592653589793`, `atan2f`, `cosf`, and `sinf`.
Added small fake `HSD_JObjSet*` inline functions that directly assign JObj transform fields and conditionally call `ftCo_800C6AFC`, marked as temporary until another library area is matched.
Used item state table callback shape (`ItemStateTable it_803F6A28[5]`) to anchor the module's state functions and match item engine expectations.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; observations are based on the title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that remove `fake_HSD_JObjSetRotation`, `fake_HSD_JObjSetScale`, or `fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate` after `lbcoll` matching work.
Check whether the duplicate `it_80277040(Item_GObj*)` declaration in `it_2725.h` was later cleaned up.
Look for subsequent semantic renames of `itLinkArrowVars` fields, especially `x18`, `x24`-`x48`, `x54`-`x84`, `x94`, `xA0`, `xB4`, and `xE0`.
Compare later `itlinkarrow.c` revisions for whether `ftCo_80094098` became implemented or further renamed.
Search for PRs that improve Link arrow reflection, collision, or trail rendering states around `it_802A9A80`, `it_802A9B08`, `it_802A9CE0`, and `it_802A9D4C`.
